name: MCRD Parris Island
tagline: The legendary Marine Corps recruit depot in South Carolina's Lowcountry, where Marines are made.
_note: Marine Corps Recruit Depot (Port Royal/Beaufort, SC Lowcountry). CONUS full template (BAH MHA SC258 Beaufort/Parris Island; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Trains recruits from east of the Mississippi and all female recruits. Population = recruits + drill instructors + cadre/staff. Nearby MCAS Beaufort. Medical: Naval Hospital Beaufort. Facts verified from official sources (mcrdpi.marines.mil, Naval Hospital Beaufort, DeCA/MCX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). South Carolina is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 29906 (Beaufort).

data.Trains: Recruits from east of the Mississippi and all female Marine recruits
data.Nearby: MCAS Beaufort, a short drive away

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Marine Corps Recruit Depot Parris Island is one of the two places that make Marines, training recruits from east of the Mississippi River and all female recruits through the Corps' demanding boot camp.

It sits in South Carolina's Lowcountry near Beaufort, a historic, scenic coastal area. For families, this is typically a tour as a drill instructor, instructor, or support staff, set in a warm, marshy, beautiful corner of the South.

data.subtitle: This is usually a drill-instructor or staff tour, often demanding on time. The main choice is which Beaufort-area community fits your commute and family, since recruits live on the depot.
intro: Family housing decisions here are for staff and cadre (recruits are berthed on the depot). The Beaufort area is a charming, moderately priced Lowcountry community, and on-base housing is available.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Beaufort, Port Royal, and the surrounding Lowcountry, with the coast and Hilton Head nearby.

recommendations.advice: Port Royal and Beaufort sit nearest the depot for the shortest commute, important on a demanding DI schedule.
Beaufort and the surrounding Lowcountry towns are family-friendly with a relaxed pace.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the local market.
Historic Beaufort, the marshes, and nearby beaches and Hilton Head make for a beautiful setting.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center. Access is busier around recruit graduations (family day and graduation).
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official MCRD Parris Island site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: MCRD Parris Island has on-base family housing for staff and cadre (recruits are berthed separately on the depot), with additional Marine housing in the Beaufort area.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA SC258 (Beaufort/Parris Island)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Beaufort County School District serves the area around the depot.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Primary and specialty outpatient care with some inpatient services, serving the depot and MCAS Beaufort; complex care via the regional network

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and around Beaufort the call depends on your tour length and the Lowcountry market.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
A demanding DI or staff schedule makes a short commute and low hassle a priority.
You want time to learn the area before committing.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The moderate Lowcountry market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a town and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Beaufort is moderately priced and charming, so buying can pencil out on a longer tour, but factor in coastal insurance and your schedule. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier, especially before a demanding training schedule starts.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on Beaufort and Port Royal before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and South Carolina vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Beaufort County School District early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Naval Hospital Beaufort or network providers.

data.a: It's a Marine Corps Recruit Depot, one of two places that make Marines, training recruits from east of the Mississippi and all female recruits. For families, it's usually a drill-instructor or staff tour.
Staff and cadre live in on-base housing or around Beaufort and Port Royal (recruits are berthed on the depot).

data.q: Can families visit during boot camp?
data.a: Yes, around graduation (family day and graduation), when access is busier. Check current dates and access rules with the depot.
Parris Island is MHA SC258 (Beaufort/Parris Island). Use the calculator above for your grade.
